cwe: CWE-1188 - Initialization of a Resource with an Insecure Default

threat.title: AWS CloudFront Origin Shield Bypass for Direct Origin DDoS
threat.description: Attackers discover the origin server IP address through DNS history, SSL certificates, or misconfigured headers, then bypass CloudFront CDN protections to launch direct DDoS attacks against the origin infrastructure (S3 buckets, ALB, EC2). This bypasses CloudFront's DDoS protection and rate limiting, overwhelming the origin server with traffic that never touches the CDN edge locations.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker performs DNS history lookup using SecurityTrails or Shodan to find origin IP address
2. Validates origin IP by checking SSL certificate SANs or HTTP headers (X-Forwarded-For, Server)
3. Scans origin IP directly to confirm accessibility (finds S3 bucket URL or ALB endpoint)
4. Launches volumetric DDoS attack directly against origin IP, bypassing CloudFront
5. Origin server becomes overwhelmed as it lacks CloudFront's DDoS protection
6. Application becomes unavailable; CloudFront edge caches expire, service fully degraded
prerequisites: Origin server IP address is publicly accessible
No custom origin headers configured for verification
Security groups allow traffic from 0.0.0.0/0 instead of CloudFront IP ranges
S3 bucket has public access or predictable bucket name
DNS history reveals pre-CloudFront origin IP addresses
SSL certificates expose origin domains in Subject Alternative Names

cve_references.description: Container escape vulnerability used in conjunction with exposed origin servers

real_world_breaches.incident: GitHub DDoS Attack via Memcached Amplification
real_world_breaches.impact: 1.35 Tbps DDoS attack, largest recorded at the time, caused service disruption
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Exposed origin servers allowing direct attacks bypassing CDN protection

real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple companies had S3 buckets accessed directly, bypassing CloudFront
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Publicly accessible S3 buckets with predictable names, no origin access control

real_world_breaches.impact: Research showed 70% of sites using CDNs had discoverable origin IPs
real_world_breaches.root_cause: DNS history, SSL certificate transparency logs, and misconfigured headers

real_world_breaches.impact: Multi-terabit DDoS attacks targeting cloud origins directly
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Attackers used Shodan and Censys to discover origin IPs, bypassed CDN protections

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Origin server IP disclosure via DNS history allowing direct DDoS bypass of CloudFront
S3 bucket direct access bypassing CloudFront origin access control

security_research.title: AWS CloudFront Security Best Practices
security_research.key_finding: Origin IP disclosure is a top security risk for CDN deployments, requiring multi-layer protection

security_research.title: CDN Origin Discovery Techniques
security_research.key_finding: 85% of CDN-protected sites have discoverable origin IPs through passive reconnaissance

mitigations.title: Implement CloudFront Origin Shield with Custom Headers
mitigations.description: Deploy CloudFront Origin Shield in a dedicated AWS region closest to your origin, and configure custom secret headers that the origin validates. This creates a mandatory checkpoint that all traffic must pass through, preventing direct origin access.

code_example.code:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'CloudFront Distribution with Origin Shield and Custom Headers'

Parameters:
  OriginDomainName:
    Type: String
    Description: 'ALB or S3 origin domain name'
  CustomHeaderSecret:
    Type: String
    NoEcho: true
    Description: 'Secret value for X-Origin-Verify header (store in Secrets Manager)'
    MinLength: 32

Resources:
  CloudFrontDistribution:
    Type: 'AWS::CloudFront::Distribution'
    Properties:
      DistributionConfig:
        Enabled: true
        Comment: 'Distribution with Origin Shield protection'
        DefaultCacheBehavior:
          TargetOriginId: ProtectedOrigin
          ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
          AllowedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
            - OPTIONS
            - PUT
            - POST
            - PATCH
            - DELETE
          CachedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
            - OPTIONS
          Compress: true
          ForwardedValues:
            QueryString: true
            Headers:
              - Host
              - CloudFront-Forwarded-Proto
            Cookies:
              Forward: all
        Origins:
          - Id: ProtectedOrigin
            DomainName: !Ref OriginDomainName
            CustomOriginConfig:
              HTTPPort: 80
              HTTPSPort: 443
              OriginProtocolPolicy: https-only
              OriginSSLProtocols:
                - TLSv1.2
              OriginReadTimeout: 30
              OriginKeepaliveTimeout: 5
            OriginShield:
              Enabled: true
              OriginShieldRegion: us-east-1  # Choose region closest to origin
            OriginCustomHeaders:
              - HeaderName: X-Origin-Verify
                HeaderValue: !Ref CustomHeaderSecret
              - HeaderName: X-CloudFront-Distribution-ID
                HeaderValue: !Ref CloudFrontDistribution
        PriceClass: PriceClass_All
        ViewerCertificate:
          CloudFrontDefaultCertificate: true
        HttpVersion: http2and3
        IPV6Enabled: true

  # WAF for additional origin protection
  CloudFrontWebACL:
    Type: 'AWS::WAFv2::WebACL'
    Properties:
      Scope: CLOUDFRONT
      DefaultAction:
        Allow: {}
      Rules:
        - Name: RateLimitRule
          Priority: 1
          Statement:
            RateBasedStatement:
              Limit: 2000
              AggregateKeyType: IP
          Action:
            Block:
              CustomResponse:
                ResponseCode: 429
          VisibilityConfig:
            SampledRequestsEnabled: true
            CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
            MetricName: RateLimitRule
      VisibilityConfig:
        SampledRequestsEnabled: true
        CloudWatchMetricsEnabled: true
        MetricName: CloudFrontWebACL

Outputs:
  DistributionDomain:
    Value: !GetAtt CloudFrontDistribution.DomainName
    Description: 'CloudFront distribution domain name'
  DistributionId:
    Value: !Ref CloudFrontDistribution
    Description: 'CloudFront distribution ID'

tools.name: AWS CloudFront Origin Shield
tools.cost: $0.01 per 10,000 requests + standard CloudFront data transfer costs

tools.name: AWS WAF
tools.cost: $5/month per web ACL + $1/month per rule + $0.60 per million requests

iso27001_controls: A.14.1.2 - Securing application services on public networks
nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Generate cryptographically random secret (32+ chars) for X-Origin-Verify header
Store secret in AWS Secrets Manager with rotation enabled
Deploy CloudFront distribution with Origin Shield enabled
Configure custom origin headers in CloudFront
Update origin security groups to allow only CloudFront IP ranges
Test direct origin access is blocked without custom header
Verify CloudFront traffic reaches origin successfully

mitigations.title: Restrict Origin Access with VPC Security Groups and CloudFront IP Ranges
mitigations.description: Lock down origin server security groups to only accept traffic from CloudFront's managed IP prefix list. This prevents direct attacks on the origin infrastructure.

code_example.code:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Origin ALB with CloudFront-only access'

Resources:
  # Application Load Balancer
  OriginALB:
    Type: 'AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer'
    Properties:
      Name: origin-alb
      Scheme: internet-facing
      Type: application
      IpAddressType: ipv4
      Subnets:
        - !Ref PublicSubnet1
        - !Ref PublicSubnet2
      SecurityGroups:
        - !Ref ALBSecurityGroup

  # Security Group allowing ONLY CloudFront IPs
  ALBSecurityGroup:
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup'
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: 'Allow traffic only from CloudFront'
      VpcId: !Ref VPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        # HTTPS from CloudFront managed prefix list
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          SourcePrefixListId: !Ref CloudFrontPrefixList
        # HTTP from CloudFront (redirect to HTTPS)
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          SourcePrefixListId: !Ref CloudFrontPrefixList
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: cloudfront-only-alb-sg

  # AWS managed prefix list for CloudFront IPs
  CloudFrontPrefixList:
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::PrefixList'
    Properties:
      PrefixListName: cloudfront-ips
      AddressFamily: IPv4
      MaxEntries: 60
      Entries:
        # These are examples - use actual CloudFront IPs from:
        # aws ec2 describe-managed-prefix-lists --filters Name=prefix-list-name,Values=com.amazonaws.global.cloudfront.origin-facing
        - Cidr: 13.32.0.0/15
          Description: 'CloudFront IP range 1'
        - Cidr: 13.35.0.0/16
          Description: 'CloudFront IP range 2'
        - Cidr: 52.84.0.0/15
          Description: 'CloudFront IP range 3'
        - Cidr: 64.252.64.0/18
          Description: 'CloudFront IP range 4'
        - Cidr: 99.84.0.0/16
          Description: 'CloudFront IP range 5'
        - Cidr: 204.246.164.0/22
          Description: 'CloudFront IP range 6'
        - Cidr: 205.251.192.0/19
          Description: 'CloudFront IP range 7'

  # Lambda function to auto-update CloudFront IP ranges
  UpdateCloudFrontIPsFunction:
    Type: 'AWS::Lambda::Function'
    Properties:
      FunctionName: update-cloudfront-ips
      Runtime: python3.11
      Handler: index.lambda_handler
      Role: !GetAtt LambdaExecutionRole.Arn
      Timeout: 60
      Environment:
        Variables:
          SECURITY_GROUP_ID: !Ref ALBSecurityGroup
      Code:
        ZipFile: |
          import json
          import boto3
          import urllib3
          import os
          
          ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
          http = urllib3.PoolManager()
          
          def lambda_handler(event, context):
              # Fetch latest CloudFront IP ranges from AWS
              response = http.request('GET', 'https://ip-ranges.amazonaws.com/ip-ranges.json')
              ip_ranges = json.loads(response.data.decode('utf-8'))
              
              # Filter CloudFront origin-facing IPs
              cloudfront_ips = [
                  item['ip_prefix'] for item in ip_ranges['prefixes']
                  if item['service'] == 'CLOUDFRONT' and item['region'] == 'GLOBAL'
              ]
              
              sg_id = os.environ['SECURITY_GROUP_ID']
              
              # Get current rules
              current_sg = ec2.describe_security_groups(GroupIds=[sg_id])['SecurityGroups'][0]
              
              # Revoke old CloudFront rules
              if current_sg['IpPermissions']:
                  ec2.revoke_security_group_ingress(
                      GroupId=sg_id,
                      IpPermissions=current_sg['IpPermissions']
                  )
              
              # Add updated CloudFront IPs
              permissions = [
                  {
                      'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
                      'FromPort': 443,
                      'ToPort': 443,
                      'IpRanges': [{'CidrIp': ip, 'Description': 'CloudFront'} for ip in cloudfront_ips]
                  },
                  {
                      'IpProtocol': 'tcp',
                      'FromPort': 80,
                      'ToPort': 80,
                      'IpRanges': [{'CidrIp': ip, 'Description': 'CloudFront'} for ip in cloudfront_ips]
                  }
              ]
              
              ec2.authorize_security_group_ingress(
                  GroupId=sg_id,
                  IpPermissions=permissions
              )
              
              return {
                  'statusCode': 200,
                  'body': json.dumps(f'Updated {len(cloudfront_ips)} CloudFront IP ranges')
              }

  LambdaExecutionRole:
    Type: 'AWS::IAM::Role'
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - 'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole'
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: UpdateSecurityGroups
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: '2012-10-17'
            Statement:
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - 'ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups'
                  - 'ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress'
                  - 'ec2:RevokeSecurityGroupIngress'
                Resource: '*'

  # EventBridge rule to run daily
  DailyUpdateSchedule:
    Type: 'AWS::Events::Rule'
    Properties:
      Description: 'Update CloudFront IPs daily'
      ScheduleExpression: 'rate(1 day)'
      State: ENABLED
      Targets:
        - Arn: !GetAtt UpdateCloudFrontIPsFunction.Arn
          Id: UpdateCloudFrontIPsTarget

  LambdaInvokePermission:
    Type: 'AWS::Lambda::Permission'
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Ref UpdateCloudFrontIPsFunction
      Action: 'lambda:InvokeFunction'
      Principal: events.amazonaws.com
      SourceArn: !GetAtt DailyUpdateSchedule.Arn

Outputs:
  ALBSecurityGroupId:
    Value: !Ref ALBSecurityGroup
    Description: 'Security Group ID restricting access to CloudFront'
  LoadBalancerDNS:
    Value: !GetAtt OriginALB.DNSName
    Description: 'ALB DNS name (use as CloudFront origin)'

nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Deploy security group with CloudFront IP prefix list
Test direct access to origin IP is blocked (timeout or connection refused)
Verify CloudFront distribution can reach origin successfully
Deploy Lambda function to auto-update CloudFront IP ranges
Test Lambda function execution and security group updates
Schedule EventBridge rule for daily updates
Monitor CloudWatch Logs for Lambda execution errors

mitigations.title: Enable S3 Origin Access Control (OAC) for S3 Bucket Origins
mitigations.description: Use CloudFront Origin Access Control to ensure S3 buckets can only be accessed through CloudFront, preventing direct S3 URL access and bucket enumeration attacks.

code_example.code:
: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'CloudFront with S3 Origin Access Control'

Resources:
  # S3 Bucket for origin content
  OriginBucket:
    Type: 'AWS::S3::Bucket'
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub 'origin-content-${AWS::AccountId}'
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: DeleteOldVersions
            Status: Enabled
            NoncurrentVersionExpirationInDays: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: Purpose
          Value: CloudFront Origin

  # Origin Access Control (OAC) - Replaces legacy OAI
  CloudFrontOriginAccessControl:
    Type: 'AWS::CloudFront::OriginAccessControl'
    Properties:
      OriginAccessControlConfig:
        Name: !Sub 'OAC-${OriginBucket}'
        Description: 'Origin Access Control for S3 bucket'
        SigningBehavior: always
        SigningProtocol: sigv4
        OriginAccessControlOriginType: s3

  # CloudFront Distribution
  CloudFrontDistribution:
    Type: 'AWS::CloudFront::Distribution'
    Properties:
      DistributionConfig:
        Enabled: true
        Comment: 'Distribution with S3 OAC protection'
        DefaultRootObject: index.html
        DefaultCacheBehavior:
          TargetOriginId: S3Origin
          ViewerProtocolPolicy: redirect-to-https
          AllowedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
            - OPTIONS
          CachedMethods:
            - GET
            - HEAD
            - OPTIONS
          Compress: true
          ForwardedValues:
            QueryString: false
            Cookies:
              Forward: none
          MinTTL: 0
          DefaultTTL: 86400
          MaxTTL: 31536000
        Origins:
          - Id: S3Origin
            DomainName: !GetAtt OriginBucket.RegionalDomainName
            S3OriginConfig:
              OriginAccessIdentity: ''  # Empty for OAC (not using legacy OAI)
            OriginAccessControlId: !Ref CloudFrontOriginAccessControl
            OriginShield:
              Enabled: true
              OriginShieldRegion: us-east-1
        CustomErrorResponses:
          - ErrorCode: 403
            ResponseCode: 404
            ResponsePagePath: /404.html
            ErrorCachingMinTTL: 300
          - ErrorCode: 404
            ResponseCode: 404
            ResponsePagePath: /404.html
            ErrorCachingMinTTL: 300
        PriceClass: PriceClass_100
        ViewerCertificate:
          CloudFrontDefaultCertificate: true

  # S3 Bucket Policy allowing CloudFront OAC
  OriginBucketPolicy:
    Type: 'AWS::S3::BucketPolicy'
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref OriginBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowCloudFrontOAC
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: cloudfront.amazonaws.com
            Action: 's3:GetObject'
            Resource: !Sub '${OriginBucket.Arn}/*'
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                'AWS:SourceArn': !Sub 'arn:aws:cloudfront::${AWS::AccountId}:distribution/${CloudFrontDistribution}'
          - Sid: DenyDirectAccess
            Effect: Deny
            Principal: '*'
            Action: 's3:GetObject'
            Resource: !Sub '${OriginBucket.Arn}/*'
            Condition:
              StringNotEquals:
                'AWS:SourceArn': !Sub 'arn:aws:cloudfront::${AWS::AccountId}:distribution/${CloudFrontDistribution}'

  # CloudWatch Alarm for high error rate
  CloudFrontErrorAlarm:
    Type: 'AWS::CloudWatch::Alarm'
    Properties:
      AlarmName: !Sub 'CloudFront-High-Error-Rate-${CloudFrontDistribution}'
      AlarmDescription: 'Alerts when CloudFront error rate exceeds 5%'
      MetricName: 4xxErrorRate
      Namespace: AWS/CloudFront
      Statistic: Average
      Period: 300
      EvaluationPeriods: 2
      Threshold: 5
      ComparisonOperator: GreaterThanThreshold
      Dimensions:
        - Name: DistributionId
          Value: !Ref CloudFrontDistribution
      TreatMissingData: notBreaching

Outputs:
  DistributionDomain:
    Value: !GetAtt CloudFrontDistribution.DomainName
    Description: 'CloudFront distribution domain'
  BucketName:
    Value: !Ref OriginBucket
    Description: 'S3 origin bucket name'
  OriginAccessControlId:
    Value: !Ref CloudFrontOriginAccessControl
    Description: 'Origin Access Control ID'

iso27001_controls: A.9.1.2 - Access to networks and network services
validation_steps: Create S3 bucket with Block Public Access enabled
Deploy CloudFront Origin Access Control resource
Configure CloudFront distribution to use OAC
Apply S3 bucket policy allowing only CloudFront service principal
Test direct S3 URL access is denied (403 Forbidden)
Verify CloudFront can retrieve objects successfully
Attempt to enumerate bucket contents directly (should fail)
Monitor CloudWatch metrics for 4xx errors

mitigations.title: Implement Origin Header Validation at Application Layer
mitigations.description: Validate the custom X-Origin-Verify header at the application or web server level to reject any requests that don't originate from CloudFront, even if they bypass network-level protections.
iso27001_controls: A.9.1.2 - Access to networks and network services
nist_csf: PR.PT-1 - Audit/log records are determined and documented
validation_steps: Implement origin validation middleware in application code
Configure environment variables for secret and distribution ID
Test direct origin access without headers (should return 403)
Test with incorrect X-Origin-Verify header (should return 403)
Test with valid CloudFront headers (should succeed)
Monitor application logs for validation failures
Set up alerts for high volume of 403 errors

indicators: High volume of direct origin requests bypassing CloudFront
Unusual traffic patterns from non-CloudFront IP ranges
Spike in 4xx errors from CloudFront origin requests
Direct S3 bucket access attempts via bucket URL
DNS queries for origin server hostnames
Increased bandwidth usage at origin without corresponding CloudFront traffic
Connection attempts to origin IP addresses not routed through CloudFront

log_queries.query:
# Detect direct origin access bypassing CloudFront
fields @timestamp, @message, httpRequest.clientIp, httpRequest.uri
| filter @message like /403/ or @message like /direct access/
| filter httpRequest.headers.x-origin-verify not like /.+/
| stats count() as DirectAccessAttempts by httpRequest.clientIp
| filter DirectAccessAttempts > 10
| sort DirectAccessAttempts desc

// Detect CloudFront origin bypass attempts
AWSCloudTrail
| where EventName == "GetObject" and RequestParameters contains "s3.amazonaws.com"
| where SourceIpAddress !in (dynamic(["CloudFront IP ranges"]))
| where UserAgent !contains "CloudFront"
| summarize AttemptCount = count() by SourceIpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where AttemptCount > 20
| order by AttemptCount desc

index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudfront:accesslogs
| search sc_status=403 OR sc_status=503
| search NOT x_origin_verify=*
| stats count as AccessDenied by c_ip, cs_uri_stem
| where AccessDenied > 50
| table _time, c_ip, cs_uri_stem, AccessDenied
| sort -AccessDenied

event.dataset: "aws.cloudfront" AND http.response.status_code: (403 OR 503) AND NOT cloudfront.origin_verify: *
| stats count by source.ip
| where count > 100

# Detect ALB traffic not from CloudFront
fields @timestamp, client_ip, request_url, elb_status_code
| filter client_ip not like /^(13\.32\.|13\.35\.|52\.84\.|64\.252\.64\.|99\.84\.|204\.246\.164\.|205\.251\.192\.)/
| filter request_http_header_x_origin_verify not like /.+/
| stats count() as DirectRequests by client_ip
| filter DirectRequests > 100
| sort DirectRequests desc

tools.name: AWS GuardDuty
tools.usage: Monitors for unusual API activity and unauthorized network probes against origins

tools.name: AWS CloudWatch Alarms
tools.usage: Set alarms for high 4xx error rates, origin connection failures, and traffic anomalies

tools.name: Shodan
tools.usage: Proactively search for exposed origin IPs and services using Shodan API

tools.name: SecurityTrails
tools.usage: Monitor DNS history to identify if origin IPs were exposed before CloudFront deployment
